agentsTable.schema:
CREATE TABLE agents (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    record_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    agent_index INTEGER NOT NULL,
    agent_raw TEXT NOT NULL,
    agent_type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(agent_type IN ('personal', 'corporate', 'meeting')),
    role_raw TEXT,
    role_source TEXT,
    authority_uri TEXT,
    agent_norm TEXT NOT NULL,
    agent_confidence REAL NOT NULL CHECK(agent_confidence BETWEEN 0 AND 1),
    agent_method TEXT NOT NULL,
    agent_notes TEXT,
    role_norm TEXT NOT NULL,
    role_confidence REAL NOT NULL CHECK(role_confidence BETWEEN 0 AND 1),
    role_method TEXT NOT NULL,
    provenance_json TEXT NOT NULL,
    FOREIGN KEY (record_id) REFERENCES records(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
)

sampleData.agent_norm: faïtlovitch collection. ancient ethiopic manuscripts collection

agentsTable.wordOrderPattern: surname, given (Western names in inverted order; Hebrew names in direct order with patronymic: given ben father)

publisherAuthorities.schema:
CREATE TABLE publisher_authorities (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    canonical_name TEXT NOT NULL,
    canonical_name_lower TEXT NOT NULL,
    type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(type IN ('printing_house', 'private_press', 'modern_publisher', 'bibliophile_society', 'unknown_marker', 'unresearched')),
    dates_active TEXT,
    date_start INTEGER,
    date_end INTEGER,
    location TEXT,
    notes TEXT,
    sources TEXT,
    confidence REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.5,
    is_missing_marker INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    viaf_id TEXT,
    wikidata_id TEXT,
    cerl_id TEXT,
    branch TEXT,
    primary_language TEXT,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL
)
publisherAuthorities.variantPattern: Two-table normalized authority system: publisher_authorities holds canonical identities (one row per publisher) with external IDs (VIAF, Wikidata, CERL); publisher_variants holds name forms (many-to-one FK to authorities) with script detection, language tagging, priority ordering, and case-insensitive unique index on variant_form_lower for fast lookup. Variants include Latin, Hebrew, and transliterated forms.

authorityEnrichment.schema:
CREATE TABLE authority_enrichment (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    authority_uri TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
    nli_id TEXT,
    wikidata_id TEXT,
    viaf_id TEXT,
    isni_id TEXT,
    loc_id TEXT,
    label TEXT,
    description TEXT,
    person_info TEXT,
    place_info TEXT,
    image_url TEXT,
    wikipedia_url TEXT,
    source TEXT NOT NULL,
    confidence REAL,
    fetched_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    expires_at TEXT NOT NULL
)
hebrewLabelExamples: חברת שונצינו של ידידי הספרים היהודיים
authorityEnrichment.personInfoStructure: JSON object with keys: birth_year, death_year, birth_place, death_place, nationality, occupations, description, teachers, students, notable_works

imprintsTable.schema:
CREATE TABLE imprints (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    record_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    occurrence INTEGER NOT NULL,
    date_raw TEXT,
    place_raw TEXT,
    publisher_raw TEXT,
    manufacturer_raw TEXT,
    source_tags TEXT NOT NULL,
    date_start INTEGER,
    date_end INTEGER,
    date_label TEXT,
    date_confidence REAL,
    date_method TEXT,
    place_norm TEXT,
    place_display TEXT,
    place_confidence REAL,
    place_method TEXT,
    publisher_norm TEXT,
    publisher_display TEXT,
    publisher_confidence REAL,
    publisher_method TEXT,
    country_code TEXT,
    country_name TEXT,
    FOREIGN KEY (record_id) REFERENCES records(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
)
relevantColumns: publisher_raw (raw publisher string from MARC, potential seed for agent-publisher cross-reference)
publisher_norm (normalized publisher key, links to publisher_authorities via variants)
place_raw (raw place string, contextual for agent activity location)
date_start / date_end (date range for activity period cross-reference)

problematicAgents.buxtorf: Stored as single norm 'buxtorf, johann' -- but historically there were two Johann Buxtorfs (father 1564-1629 and son 1599-1664), both Hebraists in Basel. The current schema collapses them into one agent_norm. Raw forms all appear as 'Buxtorf, Johann,' with no disambiguation. authority_uri may distinguish them but agent_norm does not.
problematicAgents.mendelssohn: Stored as single norm 'mendelssohn, moses' (11 records). Only Moses Mendelssohn appears. Raw form: 'Mendelssohn, Moses,'. No Hebrew form present despite being a major figure in Jewish Enlightenment literature.
problematicAgents.maimonides: Split across two norms that are NOT linked: 'maimonides, moses' (Latin form) and 'משה בן מימון' (Hebrew patronymic form, 15 records). These represent the same person (Rabbi Moshe ben Maimon / Rambam) but appear as separate agents. A query for 'Maimonides' would miss the 15 Hebrew-form records.
problematicAgents.karo: Stored only in Hebrew as 'קארו, יוסף בן אפרים' (13 records). No Latin/English form exists (e.g., 'Karo, Joseph' or 'Caro, Joseph'). A search for 'Joseph Karo' or 'Caro' would fail entirely. The LIKE '%karo%' query also matched unrelated agents (Beaumarchais [caron], Carolus, Caroline) showing the difficulty of substring matching.

proposedAgentAliasSchema:
CREATE TABLE agent_aliases (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    authority_id INTEGER NOT NULL,
    alias_form TEXT NOT NULL,
    alias_form_lower TEXT NOT NULL,
    script TEXT DEFAULT 'latin',
    language TEXT,
    alias_type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(alias_type IN (
        'primary',          -- the preferred display form for this script
        'variant_spelling',  -- alternate transliteration or spelling
        'cross_script',      -- same person in different script (e.g., Hebrew <-> Latin)
        'patronymic',        -- patronymic form (e.g., ben Maimon)
        'acronym',           -- rabbinic acronym (e.g., Rambam)
        'historical'         -- historical Latin or vernacular name form
    )),
    is_primary INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    priority INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
    notes TEXT,
    source TEXT,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    FOREIGN KEY (authority_id) REFERENCES agent_authorities(id) ON DELETE CASCADE
);

CREATE TABLE agent_authorities (
    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
    canonical_name TEXT NOT NULL,
    canonical_name_lower TEXT NOT NULL,
    agent_type TEXT NOT NULL CHECK(agent_type IN ('personal', 'corporate', 'meeting')),
    birth_year INTEGER,
    death_year INTEGER,
    nationality TEXT,
    occupation TEXT,
    notes TEXT,
    viaf_id TEXT,
    wikidata_id TEXT,
    nli_id TEXT,
    isni_id TEXT,
    confidence REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0.5,
    created_at TEXT NOT NULL,
    updated_at TEXT NOT NULL
);

CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_agent_auth_canonical_lower ON agent_authorities(canonical_name_lower);
CREATE INDEX idx_agent_auth_type ON agent_authorities(agent_type);
CREATE INDEX idx_agent_aliases_authority ON agent_aliases(authority_id);
CREATE UNIQUE INDEX idx_agent_aliases_form_lower ON agent_aliases(alias_form_lower);
CREATE INDEX idx_agent_aliases_script ON agent_aliases(script);
CREATE INDEX idx_agent_aliases_type ON agent_aliases(alias_type);

seedDataSources.fieldPath: agents.authority_uri JOIN authority_enrichment.authority_uri -> label, person_info (birth/death years, occupations), wikidata_id, viaf_id
seedDataSources.estimatedRecords: 2,434 distinct authority URIs with enrichment data covering 3,613 agent rows; provides external IDs and biographical metadata for seeding agent_authorities
3,102 distinct agent_norm values that can be clustered by edit distance and cross-script matching to identify same-person groups (e.g., maimonides/משה בן מימון)

seedDataSources.fieldPath: authority_enrichment.person_info JSON -> birth_year, death_year, nationality, occupations, description
seedDataSources.estimatedRecords: 5,667 enrichment records with structured biographical JSON; can populate agent_authorities biographical fields
21 Hebrew-script labels providing cross-script alias candidates for agent_aliases with alias_type='cross_script'

seedDataSources.fieldPath: agents.agent_raw -> variant spelling forms before normalization
seedDataSources.estimatedRecords: All agent rows; raw forms like 'Buxtorf, Johann,' and 'Maimonides, Moses,' provide variant_spelling aliases distinct from agent_norm

seedDataSources.fieldPath: Existing publisher authority system provides the proven two-table pattern to replicate
seedDataSources.estimatedRecords: 227 authorities / 265 variants -- serves as architectural template, not direct seed data
